rubric_en: How elderly Americans amassed disproportionate wealth and power
rubric_zh: 美国老年人如何积累了不成比例的财富与权力

paragraphs.en: This article was featured in the One Story to Read Today newsletter. Sign up for it here.
paragraphs.zh: 这篇文章刊登在 One Story to Read Today 时讯中。可在此订阅。

paragraphs.en: Gerontocracy has always thrived in undemocratic places—Communist people’s republics, Gulf monarchies—where only death could pry power from the ruling elders. American gerontocracy is exceptional for being freely elected. Donald Trump will soon be an octogenarian, and is president in part because the preceding octogenarian, Joe Biden, did not want to admit his senescence. The median senator is 65, and the oldest, 92-year-old Chuck Grassley, has not ruled out running for reelection in 2028. The typical general-election voter is a spry 52, but in primary elections, which decide the majority of political contests, that number rises to 59. Half of all the money donated to political campaigns comes from Americans age 66 and older.
paragraphs.zh: 老龄化寡头政治一向在缺乏民主的地方蓬勃发展——无论是共产主义人民共和国，还是海湾君主国——在这些地方，唯有死亡才能把权力从在位长者手中剥离。美国的老龄化寡头政治之所以特殊，是因为它是通过自由选举形成的。唐纳德·特朗普很快将成为八十多岁的长者，而部分原因是前任八十多岁的乔·拜登并不想承认自己的衰老。参议员的年龄中位数是65岁，而最年长的92岁查克·格拉斯利并未排除在2028年谋求连任。普通的普选选民平均只有52岁，但在决定多数政治竞争的初选中，这一数字上升到59岁。美国66岁及以上人群贡献了全部政治竞选资金的一半。

paragraphs.en: Although political gerontocracy has operated overtly, the rising economic power of the elderly has escaped much notice. Over the past 40 or so years, American wealth has grown ever more concentrated among the oldest generations. In 1989, Americans over age 55 held 56 percent of it; today they hold 74 percent. During that same period, the share of wealth held by Americans under 40 has shrunk by nearly half, from 12 to 6.6 percent. The color of money is now gray.
paragraphs.zh: 尽管政治上的老龄化寡头统治一直公开存在，但老年人日益增强的经济力量却鲜少被注意。过去约40年间，美国财富越来越集中在最年长的一代人手中。1989年，55岁以上美国人持有56%的财富；今天这一比例上升到74%。同一时期，40岁以下美国人持有的财富份额从12%降至6.6%，几乎减半。金钱的颜色现在是灰色。

paragraphs.en: Much of this shift is the result of demographic change: 18 percent of Americans are senior citizens today, up from 13 percent in 1990. But even at the household level, Americans over 55 have accrued wealth more rapidly than those who are younger. Among those 75 and older, the numbers are particularly striking. In 1983, their household net worth was only slightly above the national average; by 2022, it was 55 percent higher.
paragraphs.zh: 这种变化很大程度上源于人口结构变化：美国老年人口现在占18%，高于1990年的13%。但即使在家庭层面，55岁以上美国人积累的财富也增长得更快，超过年轻一代。在75岁及以上群体中，这一数字尤为引人注目。1983年，这一群体的家庭净资产仅略高于全国平均水平；到2022年，已高出55%。

paragraphs.en: For nearly a century, some of the central debates in American politics have been over inequalities—between rich and poor, male and female, Black and white. When the Baby Boomers were children, older Americans were widely viewed as vulnerable. “Fifty percent of the elderly exist below minimum standards of decency, and this is a figure much higher than that for any other age group,” Michael Harrington wrote in his 1962 book The Other America, often credited with inspiring the War on Poverty. “This is no country for old men.”
paragraphs.zh: 近一个世纪以来，美国政治中的一些核心争论一直围绕着不平等——富人和穷人、男性和女性、黑人与白人之间的差距。当“婴儿潮一代”还是孩子时，美国老年人普遍被视为弱势群体。迈克尔·哈灵顿在其1962年的著作《另一个美国》里写道，这本书常被认为激发了“反贫困战争”运动：“50%的老年人生活水平低于最低体面标准，这一比例远高于任何其他年龄群体。”“这不是一个适合老年人生活的国家。”

paragraphs.en: Three years later, in 1965, Medicare was created. A major expansion of Social Security followed in 1972. These changes were remarkably effective: The share of elderly people living in poverty dropped by more than one-third within a decade. But because these programs are broad-based entitlements, they have transferred huge sums to the prosperous, too. The portfolios of that latter group, meanwhile, have been swelled by a rising stock market and rising home values, outcomes that may not be entirely replicable for younger generations. As a result of all of these factors, intergenerational inequality between old and young has not merely reversed. It has accelerated.
paragraphs.zh: 三年后，1965年，医疗保险（Medicare）诞生。1972年，社会保障（Social Security）实现了重大扩展。这些改革非常有效：十年内，生活在贫困中的老年人比例下降了三分之一以上。但由于这类项目是面向全民的法定福利，它们也向富裕群体转移了大量资金。与此同时，后者的资产组合又被不断上涨的股市和房价推高，这种结果对年轻一代而言可能难以复制。由此，老年人与年轻人之间的代际不平等不仅仅是被逆转了，而是加速恶化。

paragraphs.en: Most current Social Security and Medicare beneficiaries will receive more from the program over their lifetime than they paid in taxes, and the extra money will necessarily come from the pockets of younger generations. The two programs now pay out more than $2 trillion a year, more than one-third of all federal expenditures. Their sustainability was a subject of major debate during the Obama years, when the national debt was much lower than it is today and interest rates on that debt were close to zero. Financially, the matter is more urgent now. The trust funds for Social Security benefits and Medicare’s hospital insurance are projected to become insolvent in roughly seven years.
paragraphs.zh: 大多数现有的社会保障和医疗保险领取者在一生中将从项目中获得的金额，超过他们所缴纳的税款，并且这部分新增资金必然来自更年轻一代的口袋。现今，这两项计划每年支出超过2万亿美元，占全部联邦支出的一部分以上（超过三分之一）。在奥巴马执政期间，这两项福利的可持续性就是一个重大争论议题；当时全国性债务远低于今天，债务利率也几乎接近零。如今，这一问题在财务上更加紧迫。社会保障福利与医疗保险住院保险的信托基金预计将在大约七年后耗尽。

paragraphs.en: Lyman Stone: The Boomers ruined everything
paragraphs.zh: 利曼·斯通：婴儿潮一代把一切都毁掉了

paragraphs.en: Yet even noticing the looming threats has become taboo for the two major political parties. One of Trump’s shrewdest political realizations was that entitlement reform—once a priority for fiscal conservatives—was a losing issue. Instead, he has pledged not to touch entitlement spending and lavished seniors with even more government money. His One Big Beautiful Bill Act created a special $6,000 tax deduction for seniors, which will cost taxpayers $91 billion over the next four years. The same bill cuts $1 trillion in spending on Medicaid, which is expected to leave some 5 million working-age Americans uninsured.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，甚至承认这些迫在眉睫的威胁也已成为两大政党的禁忌。特朗普最精明的政治判断之一就是：所谓“福利保障改革”——过去一直是财政保守派的重要议题——已成为难以赢得选民支持的话题。于是，他承诺不动福利开支，并向老年人再撒一把政府财政资金。他提出的《全民大美法案》为老年人设立了每人6,000美元的专项税收扣除，预计在未来四年内将使纳税人负担910亿美元的成本。同一部法案又削减了1万亿美元的医疗补助（Medicaid）支出，这很可能让约500万名年龄在职的人群失去保险。

paragraphs.en: This bodes poorly for intergenerational peace. Respect for elders is being replaced by resentment of elders. A majority of young Americans no longer believe in the American dream. Many Millennials and Gen Zers expressly blame the Boomers for that, accusing them of hoarding wealth, jobs, and power. Many of these accusations are inchoate, but they are not entirely baseless.
paragraphs.zh: 这预示着代际和平的前景并不乐观。对长辈的尊敬正在被对长辈的怨恨取代。大多数美国年轻人已不再相信“美国梦”。许多“千禧一代”和“Z世代”公开将责任归咎于婴儿潮一代，指责他们囤积了财富、岗位和权力。许多这样的指控虽然有些模糊，但并非全无依据。

paragraphs.en: The best rebuttal to the gerontocratic critique is that young Americans do not appreciate how good they have it. Although people of working age possess a smaller share of the national wealth, they are richer in absolute terms than Boomers were at their age. The median 35-year-old Millennial earns 38 percent more in post-tax, inflation-adjusted income than the typical Boomer did at the same age, according to research by the economist Kevin Corinth. Gen Zers have only begun their careers, but so far they are earning more than their Millennial predecessors. This trend shows up in wealth statistics, too. When Boomers were between the ages of 25 and 43, they had a median net worth of $58,000 (in 2022 dollars); Millennials at the same stage of life had a net worth of $85,000. So why are young Americans so depressed about their economic future?
paragraphs.zh: 对这种“老龄政治统治”批评的最好反驳，是年轻美国人并未真正意识到自己其实过得很好。虽然劳动年龄人群在全国财富中的占比较低，但从绝对数值上看，他们比婴儿潮一代在同龄时更富有。经济学家凯文·科林斯（Kevin Corinth）的研究显示，典型的婴儿潮一代在35岁时的税后实际收入的中位数，比当代35岁“千禧一代”要低38%。“Z世代”才刚刚起步进入职业生涯，但迄今他们的收入已经高于上一代“千禧一代”。这种趋势也体现在财富数据上：婴儿潮一代在25至43岁时的财富中位数为5.8万美元（以2022年美元计），而“千禧一代”在同一人生阶段的中位净资产则是8.5万美元。那么，为什么美国年轻人对自己的经济前景依旧如此悲观？

paragraphs.en: From the May 2023 issue: Jean M. Twenge on the myth of the broke Millennial
paragraphs.zh: 摘自2023年5月刊：让·M·特文格（Jean M. Twenge）关于“穷困潦倒”式“千禧一代”神话的观点

paragraphs.en: The pathologies of the housing market are one reason. The typical home today costs five times the median annual income, up from 3.5 times the median annual income in 1984. Boomers got lucky: When they were young, they could afford to buy houses that then appreciated fantastically in value. But that luck was arguably manufactured by Washington, which engineered the rise of 30-year, fixed-rate mortgages and created tax deductions for mortgage interest and property taxes.
paragraphs.zh: 住房市场的种种病灶是一个原因。今天的典型住房价格是收入中位数的五倍，而1984年仅为3.5倍。婴儿潮一代算是走了运：当他们年轻时，可以买得起房子，而这些房子后来又大幅增值。但这种“运气”在一定程度上是华盛顿人为制造的，它推动了30年期固定利率抵押贷款的普及，并为房贷利息和房地产税创造了税前扣除政策。

paragraphs.en: These government subsidies still exist today. But even with them, younger Americans cannot buy houses at the same rate that Boomers did. In a paper titled “Giving Up,” the economists Seung Hyeong Lee and Younggeun Yoo predicted that Millennials will enter retirement with much lower homeownership rates than the generations before them—74 percent compared with 84 percent for Boomers. Some 15 percent of Millennials, they noted, had already given up on homeownership by age 30. These Millennials, they found, work less, spend more on credit, and are more likely to buy cryptocurrency or make other risky investments. Feeling locked out of owning a house casts a malaise—one made worse by the anxiety that the welfare state they currently support will become stingier when they eventually need it.
paragraphs.zh: 这些政府补贴今天仍然存在。但即使有这些支持，年轻美国人也买不起房子，远没有婴儿潮一代那样容易。在题为《放弃》的论文中，经济学家李承炯（Seung Hyeong Lee）与柳永根（Younggeun Yoo）预测，千禧一代退休时的住房拥有率将远低于上一代——74%对84%，后者是婴儿潮一代。研究指出，大约15%的千禧一代已经在30岁前放弃了购房的打算。这些千禧一代工作时间更少，信用消费更多，也更可能购买加密货币或进行其他高风险投资。无法拥有自己的房子让人感到压抑和无力，这种情绪还因一种担忧而加重：当他们真正需要时，他们现在支持的福利国家很可能会变得更紧缩。

paragraphs.en: Older generations used the levers of government to create this situation. In high-cost cities, the building of new homes and apartment complexes is often derailed in local planning and zoning-board meetings. In 2019, the political scientists Katherine Levine Einstein, Maxwell Palmer, and David Glick published a study examining who attended such meetings in the Boston area. The attendees, they found, were likely to be longtime homeowners who oppose new development. Preventing construction kept the value of their assets high—at the expense of younger, prospective homeowners.
paragraphs.zh: 更老的一代动用了政府杠杆来制造了这种局面。在高房价城市，新建住房和公寓群的建设常常在地方规划和分区委员会会议中被卡住。2019年，政治学者卡瑟琳·莱文·爱因斯坦（Katherine Levine Einstein）、麦克斯韦尔·帕尔默（Maxwell Palmer）和戴维·格利克（David Glick）发表了一项关于波士顿地区此类会议参与者的研究。他们发现，与会者往往是长期在该地拥有房产、并反对新开发的居民。阻止建设维持了他们资产的高价值，却以年轻、准买房者为代价。

paragraphs.en: Homeowner preferences hard-coded into state constitutions decades ago now further sustain the gerontocracy. In 1978, Californians voted by referendum for Proposition 13, which severely limited the property taxes that existing homeowners would have to pay—so long as they remained in place. In one study, the law was estimated to have caused a 15 percent increase in California housing prices all by itself. As longtime homeowners profited, the lost tax revenue forced reductions in school spending.
paragraphs.zh: 几十年前被写进州宪法、并且把房主偏好“固化”下来的规定，进一步支撑了这套老龄统治。在1978年，加州选民通过公投通过第13号提案，严厉限制现有房主需要缴纳的房地产税——只要他们继续居住在原房。一项研究估算，这项法律本身就使加州住房价格上升了15%。长期房主赚得越多，税收流失也迫使学校预算支出被削减。

paragraphs.en: California is not unique, and housing is not the only means by which the older generations have effectively pulled up the ladder behind them. Preferences for the elderly over the young are a fixture of public budgets nationwide. Across all government programs—federal, state, and local—$2 are now spent on seniors for every $1 spent on children.
paragraphs.zh: 加州并非孤例，住房也不是老一辈有效地把梯子向后抽走的唯一方式。全国各地的公共预算里，对老年人的偏好对年轻人而言几乎成了既定事实。无论是联邦、州还是地方所有政府项目，如今每花 1 美元在儿童身上，就会有 2 美元花在老年人身上。

paragraphs.en: According to Tim Vlandas, an Oxford political economist, advanced democracies around the world are reaching the point of “gerontonomia”—his term for a stagnating political economy set up to prioritize elderly citizens. These citizens punish their elected governments for inflation, which lessens the value of savings and pension payments. They are much more tolerant of unemployment, because they no longer work; slow growth, because their wealth has already accumulated; and high public debt, because their descendants will pay it. The result, Vlandas argues, is lower wage growth for those still working, and also worse outcomes for their children, as a result of lower social investment over the course of their lives.
paragraphs.zh: 牛津大学政治经济学家蒂姆·弗兰达斯（Tim Vlandas）指出，世界各地的发达民主国家都在走向他所称的“老龄经济学”（gerontonomia），即一种政治经济体制停滞不前、并被设置为优先照顾老年公民的局面。这些公民会责备他们选出的政府推高通胀，因为这会降低储蓄和养老金的实际价值；他们对失业更能容忍，因为他们已不再受雇；对增长放缓更能忍受，因为他们的财富已经积累；并且对高公共债务也更宽容，因为最终还债的是他们的后代。弗兰达斯认为，其结果就是在岗人群的工资增速更低，也使他们的子女更糟糕，因为一生中公共投资更少，导致后续代际的结果更差。

paragraphs.en: Graying democracies everywhere have made generous pension commitments that they are struggling to maintain. In the United Kingdom, the “triple lock,” a rule that in most years mandates that state pension payments increase more than inflation, seems politically impossible to change. In France, people protested for months against Emmanuel Macron in 2023 over the raising of the retirement age from 62 to 64.
paragraphs.zh: 在各国都在老龄化的民主国家中，政府都做出了慷慨的退休金承诺，却正苦于维持这些承诺。以英国为例，那项名为“三重锁定（triple lock）”的规则在大多数年份都会要求国家养老金支付增长快于通胀率，这项规定在政治上几乎不可能被改变。法国在 2023 年，民众持续数月抗议埃马纽埃尔·马克龙，反对将退休年龄从 62 岁提高到 64 岁。

paragraphs.en: Yet these challenges are especially acute in America. Because Social Security still has a trust fund to draw on, voters may not realize that benefits already exceed contributions. But this fund, which stood at $2.9 trillion in 2021, is on pace to dwindle to zero by about 2033. The Trump administration’s immigration crackdown will hasten the arrival of exhaustion day by cutting the number of tax-paying workers who support retirees’ benefits. Once that trust fund is gone—absent a tax increase on current workers or some other change—beneficiaries would suffer an immediate 23 percent cut in payments. A similar process would leave the Medicare Part A program, which covers hospital stays for the elderly, insolvent at about the same time.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，这些挑战在美国尤为尖锐。由于社会保障仍有一个信托基金可供提取，选民可能没有意识到，领取福利的总额已经超过了缴费总额。但这个基金在 2021 年时为 2.9 万亿美元，按当前步伐大约到 2033 年就会耗尽。特朗普政府的移民收紧政策会加快耗尽时间，因为它会减少缴税工人数量，而这些税收是用来支持退休人员福利的。一旦信托基金用尽——若不对现役纳税人增税或实施其他改革——受益人将会立刻遭遇 23% 的支付削减。同样的过程也会在大致同一时间让覆盖老年人住院的 Medicare A 计划陷入破产。

paragraphs.en: The national debt will by then be gargantuan. Previously unthinkable ideas—such as means-tested Social Security benefits, confiscatory wealth taxes, even health-care rationing—might be contemplated. The bill coming due for the senior welfare state might not trouble this president, but it could well be the defining problem for the next one.
paragraphs.zh: 届时，美国国债将会变得庞大得惊人。此前几乎不可能被认真讨论的想法——比如按收入条件发放社会保障福利、征收征用性财产税，甚至实行医疗资源配给——都可能被拿来讨论。老年福利国家即将到期的这一账单，也许不会困扰当下这位总统，但很可能会成为下一任总统的核心难题。

paragraphs.en: Some people would like to start the fight over how to resolve it now. Among them are radical thinkers who contend that in order to defeat economic gerontocracy, Americans must first defang the elderly ruling class. In his forthcoming book, Gerontocracy in America, the 54-year-old Yale law and history professor Samuel Moyn calls for destroying this “tyranny of the minority,” set up by “old people with enormous private power who hold society in chains.” Power, he argues, needs to be seized back, leaving “the elderly divested of political power, wealth, and property.”
paragraphs.zh: 有人希望现在就开始争论如何解决这个问题。其中有些激进思想者认为，为了击败“经济上的老龄寡头政治”，美国人必须先削弱掌权的老年阶层。54岁的耶鲁大学法学与历史教授塞缪尔·莫因在即将出版的《Gerontocracy in America》一书中，呼吁摧毁由“拥有巨大私人权力、并将社会锁在锁链中的老年人”建立的“少数人暴政”。他主张，必须收回权力，让“老年人剥离政治权力、财富与财产”。

paragraphs.en: Moyn wants to create mandatory retirement ages, perhaps starting as low as 65, for elected officials and people with some desirable private-sector jobs. Then he would come for the money, increasing taxes on both income and accumulated assets to dilute the share of elderly wealth. Most astonishingly, he proposes diluting older Americans’ political power too, by literally valuing the votes of young people more, on the theory that the latter will suffer the consequences of political decisions for much longer. (This proposed social engineering is both harsh and vanishingly improbable. The modern legal principle of “one person, one vote” exists for good reason.)
paragraphs.zh: 莫因主张对当选官员和一些有吸引力的民营职位实行强制退休年龄，可能从65岁开始。他接着提出征收更多税，既包括所得税，也包括累积资产税，以稀释老年人的财富份额。更令人震惊的是，他还提出通过把年轻人的选票“实际价值”提高来稀释老年美国人的政治权力，理由是年轻人将更长期地承受政治决策的后果。（这种社会工程既严厉又几乎不可能实现。现代法律原则“一个人、一票”之所以存在，是有其充分理由的。）

paragraphs.en: Still, Moyn’s ideas underscore the need for an equitable post-Boomer settlement, one that will be easier to find if we start the search sooner rather than later. In the 20th century, the United States realized it was too rich and too decent a country to allow its elderly citizens to live in penury. And it made an enduring commitment to address that problem, one that has unequivocally succeeded: There has never been a better time to be a senior citizen in America. And yet the U.S. has made no comparable commitment to working families, who are stymied not only by expensive housing but also by child-care and higher-education bills. Child poverty in America persists at levels alarmingly higher than in other advanced democracies.
paragraphs.zh: 尽管如此，莫因的观点突出了对一个更公平的“后婴儿潮一代”安排的迫切需要，而这种安排越早开始寻求，越容易达成。20世纪，美国意识到自己过于富裕和过于“体面”，不应让年迈公民生活在贫困中。于是它作出了一个持久承诺来处理这个问题，这一承诺毫无疑问取得了成功：美国历史上从未有过对老年人更有利的时代。尽管如此，美国却没有对工薪家庭做出同等程度的承诺。工薪家庭不仅被高昂的住房成本困住，还被育儿和高等教育的账单所掣肘。美国的儿童贫困率持续居高不下，远高于其他发达民主国家。

paragraphs.en: Initiatives such as Franklin D. Roosevelt’s New Deal and Lyndon B. Johnson’s Great Society established what current workers in a decent country owed to retirees. A new social contract can be struck that would deliver at least some optimism for today’s workers. Curing gerontonomia would require redirecting some public funds from programs aimed at the elderly, such as Social Security, to family benefits, education, and infrastructure. But an intergenerational recalibration can come about in gentler ways than Moyn’s: The wealthiest Social Security recipients, for instance, could forgo some of their scheduled benefits, which could instead be contributed annually to “baby bond” accounts for America’s children, a source of capital to be used in adulthood.
paragraphs.zh: 像富兰克林·D·罗斯福的“新政”和林登·约翰逊的“伟大社会”这样的举措，确立了在一个体面的国家里在职劳动者对退休者应尽的责任。完全可以重新签订一份新的社会契约，这将给当代劳动者带来至少一些希望。要治愈“老龄主义”（gerontonomia），需要将部分公共资金从面向老年人的项目（例如社会保障）转向家庭福利、教育和基础设施。不过，跨代再平衡可以比莫因所主张的方式更温和：例如，社会保障最富有的领取者可以放弃部分既定给付，这些资金每年改为存入“婴儿债券”账户，作为美国儿童日后成年时可使用的资本来源。

paragraphs.en: And not every solution rests with the welfare state. Cutting through housing restrictions would generate enormous social benefit, if America’s elderly ruling class were to allow such a feat. Today’s gerontocrats will eventually die. But their legacy will be a mess to sort out.
paragraphs.zh: 而且并非所有解决方案都要依赖福利国家。如果美国的老年统治阶层愿意允许突破住房限制，社会将获得巨大的整体收益。今天的老龄统治者终将离世，但他们留下的遗产将是一团难以梳理的烂摊子。

paragraphs.en: This article appears in the May 2026 print edition with the headline “A Fine Country for Old Men.”
paragraphs.zh: 本文发表于2026年5月纸媒版，标题是“老年人的理想国”。
